preamble: The transformation of the voiced labiodental fricative [v] (or its semivowel counterpart [w]) into the high front vowel [i] is a documented form of vocalization and diphthongization, often mediated by palatal segments or through complex vowel-consonant alternations in languages like Korean and various Semitic dialects.

examples.note: Historical shifts where initial labial-velar segments were replaced or vocalized into front vowels in specific phonetic environments (e.g., Barth's Law variations).
Development of the front rounded vowel into a diphthong containing the high front vowel [i].

sources: Phonological research on Semitic and Korean vowel-consonant shifts.
